title: Brazil’s cooperatives show how local communities can drive the climate transition
content: From low-carbon farming to community energy and Amazon restoration, Brazil’s cooperative sector is mobilizing millions to act on climate at a local level. The model highlights how existing co-op networks could be scaled to support a more just and resilient transition.

creator: Bernard Marszalek
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: What an overlooked oil protocol reveals about managing resource decline: An interview with Richard Heinberg
content: 20 years after a global proposal to limit oil extraction, Richard Heinberg revisits its relevance in this interview and argues that equitable rationing may be key to reducing conflict and managing resource decline.

creator: Manuel Casal Lodeiro

title: What Could Possibly Go Right?: In conversation with writer and climate activist Katharine Wilkinson
content: Dr. Katharine Wilkinson is an author, strategist, and teacher, working to heal the planet we call home. She addresses the question of “What Could Possibly Go Right?”
snippet: Dr. Katharine Wilkinson is an author, strategist, and teacher, working to heal the planet we call home. She addresses the question of “What Could Possibly Go Right?”
creator: Vicki Robin
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Rethinking self-importance in a time of social and ecological collapse
content: Across cultures, practices that limit ego and hierarchy help sustain cooperation and trust. In an era of cascading crises, rediscovering these “social technologies” could strengthen community resilience and collective action.
snippet: Across cultures, practices that limit ego and hierarchy help sustain cooperation and trust. In an era of cascading crises, rediscovering these “social technologies” could strengthen community resilien
creator: Guy Dauncey

title: Forest gardening for resilience: Growing regenerative food systems in New Zealand
content: In New Zealand, forest gardening is being reshaped to fit local climates, ecosystems and cultural contexts. Drawing on years of research and practice, this work shows how place-based adaptation can support more resilient, regenerative food systems.
snippet: In New Zealand, forest gardening is being reshaped to fit local climates, ecosystems and cultural contexts. Drawing on years of research and practice, this work shows how place-based adaptation can su
creator: Gary Marshall
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: The 2026 energy crisis and our Wile E. Coyote moment
content: For the past couple of decades, we at Post Carbon Institute have been pointing out that a transition to alternative energy sources will necessarily be slow and incomplete. Given that oil is a depleting, polluting, non-renewable resource, industrial society is due for a reckoning. We are all in an extended Wile E. Coyote moment.

creator: Richard Heinberg

title: A non-prepper’s guide to surviving collapse
content: As housing costs rise and populations age, underused space in existing homes offers an overlooked solution. In-home suites can provide affordable housing, support aging in place, and strengthen community ties while making better use of what we already have.
snippet: As housing costs rise and populations age, underused space in existing homes offers an overlooked solution. In-home suites can provide affordable housing, support aging in place, and strengthen commun
creator: Vicki Robin
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Revolt, reform or rebuild: Building resilient food systems from the ground up
content: The global food system is both essential and unsustainable, locked into patterns that resist meaningful reform. Real change, the author argues, lies in rebuilding local, regenerative food systems that can gradually replace what no longer works.
snippet: The global food system is both essential and unsustainable, locked into patterns that resist meaningful reform. Real change, the author argues, lies in rebuilding local, regenerative food systems that
creator: Gary Marshall
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: As rising seas threaten nations like Tuvalu, what does survival without land look like?
content: As low-lying island states face the loss of their territory, new legal and political strategies are emerging to sustain nations in exile. Whether they can preserve sovereignty, identity and cohesion without land remains an open question.
snippet: As low-lying island states face the loss of their territory, new legal and political strategies are emerging to sustain nations in exile. Whether they can preserve sovereignty, identity and cohesion w
creator: Ben Shread-Hewitt

title: Plastic waste is reshaping flood protection in cities
content: Plastic waste is no longer just a pollution issue. In many cities, it is now shaping how drainage systems function, with growing efforts to intercept debris upstream before it overwhelms flood infrastructure.
snippet: Plastic waste is no longer just a pollution issue. In many cities, it is now shaping how drainage systems function, with growing efforts to intercept debris upstream before it overwhelms flood infrast
creator: Monty Simus
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: In the Rising Tide, Episode 3. Gerald Barekye: Climate Justice from the Ground Up
content: We travel to Uganda to speak with human rights defender, environmental activist, and community organizer, Gerald Barekye. Gerald works with communities in oil-affected regions to support those most impacted by environmental degradation and fight for both ecological and human rights.
snippet: We travel to Uganda to speak with human rights defender, environmental activist, and community organizer, Gerald Barekye. Gerald works with communities in oil-affected regions to support those most im
creator: Alex Leff
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: As nature terms disappear, so does our ability to see and protect the natural world
content: Studies show a steep decline in nature-related language, from books to music. Now, projects like children’s books, citizen science platforms and everyday practices like gardening and birdwatching are helping rebuild our ecological vocabulary.
snippet: Studies show a steep decline in nature-related language, from books to music. Now, projects like children’s books, citizen science platforms and everyday practices like gardening and birdwatching are
creator: Reynard Loki
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Understanding how plants pause and restart growth can help develop climate‑resilient crops
content: New research shows that plants can temporarily halt root growth under stresses like cold and drought, then rapidly restart it once conditions improve. By identifying the genes behind this “pause and push” response, scientists hope to develop crops that recover more quickly from extreme weather, strengthening food security in a changing climate.

creator: Arif Ashraf

title: In conversation: Dave Murphy and Tom Murphy – if modernity can’t last forever, what comes next?
content: If modernity cannot endure within planetary limits, what comes next? Dave Murphy and Tom Murphy debate the likelihood of collapse, the possibility of adaptation, and what we owe each other in a time of ecological overshoot.
snippet: If modernity cannot endure within planetary limits, what comes next? Dave Murphy and Tom Murphy debate the likelihood of collapse, the possibility of adaptation, and what we owe each other in a time o
creator: Tom Murphy

title: What to expect from the first Conference on Transitioning Away from Fossil Fuels
content: More than 50 countries are meeting in Colombia to explore how economies can move away from coal, oil and gas through "complementary" multilateral negotiations.
snippet: More than 50 countries are meeting in Colombia to explore how economies can move away from coal, oil and gas through "complementary" multilateral negotiations.
creator: Kyla Tienhaara
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: How losing nature from our language changes how we think about the world
content: As references to rivers, trees, and wildlife fade from books, songs, and everyday speech, our connection to the natural world also diminishes. Reclaiming these words can help us recognize, appreciate, and ultimately, preserve the environment.

creator: Reynard Loki

title: On a small island in Washington, the case for conservation as stewardship
content: A rare prairie ecosystem shaped by humans in Washington State exemplifies a shift in how conservationists envision our relationship with the natural world.
snippet: A rare prairie ecosystem shaped by humans in Washington State exemplifies a shift in how conservationists envision our relationship with the natural world.
creator: Emma Marris
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: In conversation: Dave Murphy and Tom Murphy – can modernity survive planetary limits?
content: In a wide-ranging exchange, physicist Tom Murphy and energy scholar Dave Murphy explore the tension between optimism and planetary limits, debating whether modernity can endure or must give way to something entirely new.
